Copy Blue Print - Week 3 – Session 5
“Please watch this call” leads to what the avatar would say about their complaints in their own
words
Perfectly hone the language of the avatar – find ways they describe their problems
Make sure the words match the primary and secondary complaint of the avatar
If the avatar says dumped the copy needs to say dumped (think ask method)
Look for the word patterns of the avatars conversation
Post the homework in the Facebook groups
Post the niche url from the copypro app
Generate an email and copy to see how copypro works
Homework
Dovi – Diabetes niche
Starts with empathy and lots of negativity (good for cold traffic, not for warm traffic)
There is no perfect sales letter formula, the ones in copypro work most of the time
Be willing to move parts of the blueprint around
The word whine is better than complain (word alchemy)
This hurts all of the time – this could be a “diabetic curse”
Its not your fault is a good way to get people engaged
The copy can go too negative
Look for words like “body trigger” – this is the primary solution
There is also a primary cause
Use really short sentences (sometimes 2 words)
Literally is hardly needed in copy – when used it must be literal. Be careful with this word
Look to see where sentences can be chopped shorter
Guarantees and testimonials can be longer sentences.
Look for words to cut out and remove from the copy to make it more punchy
When Jon adds a word rarely to the copy
*Copywriting is rewriting
Find the enemy to the avatar and put the blame in the enemy
What caused the problem and how can it be named or blamed
Scotts copy
You have been told and evil sales lie
Redundancy is good sometimes. Be careful of how it is used.
Email solo – blank second field
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 7 --

31:30 – How the avatar has been let down…
Look for the avatar has been let down
Look for the beliefs that have led the avatar to their primary cause or pain right now.
Know the avatar and what makes them tick
Look for the bad ideas and attempts the avatar have tried:
· 	What makes them feel stupid
· 	What could be the frustration points
· 	What is the pain point for their attempts
· 	Who or what is the real enemy at this point where empathy and rapport can be built?
Use this sentence:
You probably tried ________ and it I’ll bet it let you down.
I got duped by this myself, don’t feel too bad… a lot of people do.
Weight loss example:
You probably tried cutting your fat intake down, and it probably let you down. Dont worry I did
the same thing and I got fatter…
The Samurai move is to brand the belief:
· 	Not all of them will be good
· 	They will speak the beliefs back in my words and also describe my product.
There are 4 words to focus on (from Jon) use a thesaurus to find more:
· 	Myth
· 	Lie
· 	Hoax
· 	Deception
Added by me – May not be the strongest word choice
· 	Bogus
· 	Counterfeit
· 	Fake
· 	Knock off
· 	Mock
· 	Phony
· 	Pretend
· 	Ruse
· 	Sham
· 	Trickery
(use a thesaurus and then work through word alchemy to find the best word)
34:00
This label is used to take away their fault
“You have been lied to”
“You have been deceived”

-- 2 of 7 --

Jons changes for ethical reasons:
Its not your fault, now it your responsibility. Now you have to take action.
Mistaken beliefs and branding:
You have to slave away doing hours of cardio to burn fat …
“The fat storing zone”
· 	Are you a slave to the fat storing zone?
· 	Have you been deceived by the fat storing zone?
· 	The fat storing zone is what I call the myth of the …
“list envy”
· 	“You have to have a large email list to make money”
“The looker lie”
· 	“You have to be really good looking to meet beautiful women”
The looker lie is a way to build curiosity
Look for interesting ways to phrase content for your avatar that gets their attention.
Curiosity = attention
Branded belief headlines and copy content:
· 	Did you buy into _________ (branded belief)? (I did…)
· 	__________ (branded belief) … You should be angry over this”
· 	“This trips up more __________ (avatar) than anything… I call it _________ (branded
belief)
· 	#1 mistaken belief - “This trips up more marketers than anything else out there… I call it
list envy”
· 	#10’s of mistaken beliefs - “This trips up tons of marketers all of the time.. curious as to
what it is… click here”
· 	“There is something call list envy.. I want you to click here ”
37:40
Branded beliefs:
· 	The more branded beliefs I have the better.
· 	Put the branded beliefs in emails, blog posts, ad campaigns etc..
· 	This is fodder for content
38:00 - Who is to blame
· 	People will take responsibility slowly and only after I have built trust (there is an order of
operation here)
· 	Look for a common enemy or to create one
· 	The enemy is the source of the propaganda they have bought into and the lies that have
held them back from success

-- 3 of 7 --

· 	“It isnt your fault… “__________ is the enemy of you finding success”
· 	Common sources: The media, mainstream magazines, so called experts, doctors, tv
shows … etc (pick one and not multiple)
· 	“A lot of people think it’s the so called expert dr shows or the rags like the National
Enquirer… Do you know who the main enemy is… its Opera
· 	This is border line conspiracy theory. (Just without the over the top conspiracy. These are
legitimate sources of bad ideas.
· 	Spell out the common mistaken beliefs – they will agree about what is going on
Action item to find enemies (not from the class):
· 	Who is a fake guru
· 	Where is bad information found
· 	What are some common lies and myths about the product or service
41:00 – Primary cause
This is the real cause of the prospects problems
“Do you know what is really causing you to ________(primary complaint)”
“The real cause behind all of this is ________ (one thing – primary complaint) and you fix
________ (primary complaint) everything gets better.
Its always something outside themselves but they can control or manage
This is one of the two most vital branding terms I will ever use as a copywriter, as it will set me
apart – because only I talk about it and only I have the answer for it.
I will bundle up the reality behind the cause of the prospects problem into one idea that my
prospect can grasp.
Example of building a primary cause
Primary complaint: Weight gain
Actual causes: Excessive calories, inactivity, elevated insulin … plus many more
What are their ideas that can lead to problem?
Do not lie or be deceptive
The bundled cause is: Metabolic disorder
“Do you know what the real cause of your ________ (primary complaint) its not _________
(mistaken belief) its not _________ (mistaken belief), its not _________ (mistaken belief) its not
even ________ (mistaken belief) its something I call __________ (Primary cause)
“Its something that experts everywhere is calling _________ (primary cause)”
“Its something that __________ (type of person) everywhere is calling _________ (primary
cause)
Description:
Certain genes in your body trigger your brain to crave food (its not your fault) and go into
hibernation mode (inactivity). This causes your metabolism to adapt a slower rate, which means
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 7 --

you cant burn fat. A simple fix (its simple) Ill reveal in a moment tricks these “fat genes” and
shuts down metabolic adaptation. (now the problem is easy to understand since its one thing)
46:20
Take something I know a lot about and do the same thing for my avatar.
The primary cause is a summed up version of all of the problems the avatar faces. This allows
them to focus on one thing to fix instead of a hundred. It is easier to visualize this than too many
ideas. If they can sum it up in a few sentences they can now describe their problem.
The primary solution is the cure to the primary cause – one idea or concept to fix what is going
on. Not 10 not 20 … 1 idea
Its easier to believe one thing than 2
90 % this can be doesn’t 10 % of the time it cant
48:00
Uses for a primary cause:
· 	“Something __________ (type of authority figure) in the know refer to as __________
(primary cause)”
· 	Could the real cause of your __________ (pain, problem, risk) be __________ (primary
cause)
· 	__________ (primary cause) Mystery __________ cause discovered
· 	There is only one system on earth designed to combat __________ (primary cause) from
the ground up … (place my offer here)”
Build a case one step at a time in a formula
Avatar + primary complain + secondary complaint + real problem
Tip:
I will make more sales if the avatar has an AHA moment while reading:
· 	So that is why I have never been able to ________ (primary goal)
· 	I have never thought of it that way
· 	Oh, that’s the reason why I have never been able to __________ (primary goal)
· 	I have never heard of this before
When a prospect cant go anywhere else they are drawn in and can be excited about the fix. Use
evidence to prove the idea or content.
50:00 – Questions and answers
1. 	What is a hook and how can I use them?
A hook is an idea or phrase that pulls the reader in through mystery + intrigue + cleverness (the
heart stopper gene)
Jon has a hook formula – these are in Samurai copy
Primary complaint has emotions tied in, what are they going through (frustrated, troubled,
fearful, anxious, uncertain) now they read the copy, the goal is to make them feel hope. People
buy when they feel hope (seen, heard, understand and validated)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 7 --

When a primary solution is revealed hope is developed.
Other people may be doing the action but I have my own version.
What motivates people? Fear and hope – ties them together
2. 	What are more examples of primary causes?
Look in copy pro
3. 	What is the rule for adding soon?
4. 	Is the primary goal / solution the unique mechanism
The primary solution is the unique mechanism
Primary goal: I need to lose weight, I want to feel sexy, I want to keep my marriage together
Primary solution (not the name of my product it’s the mechanism): the marriage formula, The
intimacy formula, the intimacy quadrant
The mechanism is the part of the product that gives them hope
Budweiser beer – beechwood aged (primary solution) it’s a made up term but nobody else can
use it.
P90x – muscle confusion (primary solution) (these don’t have to be complicated) they can be
word simple

anemia)

Tony Robbins is a place to start
It could be a system, quadrant
